narrative: Escalating Iran-Strait of Hormuz risks dominate the weekend tape after an 11% surge in crude, setting up a geopolitical risk premium and energy-linked CAD support for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1446, facing downside pressure as markets weigh the inflationary impact of oil against Thursday's ECB decision. USD/JPY closed Friday at 162.35; while the +264bp OIS carry advantage favors USD, the geopolitical premium is likely to trigger JPY haven flows at Sunday's open. NZD/JPY remains the critical bilateral theme (NZD 1W avg: +1.390%, JPY 1W avg: -0.489%, G10 spread: 1.879%) as RBNZ hiking bias diverges from the BoJ's accommodative -0.52% real rate.
